summary: Potassium oxalate plus sodium fluoride are chosen because they effectively prevent degradation due to cell lysis and inhibit glycolysis; this combination ensures stable glucose concentrations during extended storage before testing. The primary goal is preserving reliable results when sending a specimen specifically meant for measuring blood sugar without immediate processing constraints being an issue. To clarify further regarding other options like EDTA or heparin not typically recommended as their properties interfere more directly with glycemic assessment rather than just preservation stability concerns addressed through proper additive use described above. Summary: For blood glucose tests requiring long-term storage, using potassium oxalate combined with sodium fluoride maintains glucose concentration integrity best among available anticoagulents, ensuring accuracy upon later measurement. This dual-additive approach addresses both enzymatic activity inhibition and red blood cell hemolysis prevention crucially needed under such conditions. Other agents may compromise test reliability either via direct interference mechanisms unrelated solely towards physical/chemical stabilities pertinent herein discussed contextually appropriately
